channel.title: Cooklang Comparisons: How We Stack Up Against Other Recipe Tools on Cooklang
channel.description: Recent content in Cooklang Comparisons: How We Stack Up Against Other Recipe Tools on Cooklang

item.title: Tandoor vs Mealie vs KitchenOwl: Choosing a Self-Hosted Recipe Manager in 2026
item.author: hello@cooklang.org (Alexey)
item.description: An honest comparison of three popular self-hosted recipe managers — Tandoor, Mealie, and KitchenOwl. Setup complexity, UI, recipe import, meal planning, mobile experience, and data portability.

item.title: Mealie Review: A Developer's Honest Look at the Self-Hosted Recipe Manager
item.author: hello@cooklang.org (Alexey)
item.description: Mealie is one of the most popular self-hosted recipe managers. Here's an honest look at what it does well, where it falls short, and who it's actually for — from someone who builds a different kind of recipe tool.

item.title: Cooklang vs Mealie: Plain Text vs Self-Hosted Web App
item.author: hello@cooklang.org (Alexey)
item.description: Mealie gives you a polished self-hosted web app for your household. Cooklang gives you plain-text recipes you fully own. Honest fitness-for-purpose comparison.

item.title: Cooklang vs Tandoor: Sharp Tool vs Kitchen ERP
item.author: hello@cooklang.org (Alexey)
item.description: Tandoor packs every recipe-management feature into one self-hosted app. Cooklang does one thing — structured plain text — and lets you script the rest. Honest comparison.

item.title: Recipe Formats for Developers: Cooklang, JSON-LD, RecipeML & MealMaster (Code-Level Comparison)
item.author: hello@cooklang.org (Alexey)
item.description: A code-level comparison of the four recipe file formats you'll encounter when building recipe software: Cooklang, JSON-LD/Schema.org, RecipeML, and MealMaster. The same recipe in each format, with parser availability, data structures, and honest trade-offs.

item.title: Chef Language vs Cooklang Explained
item.author: hello@cooklang.org (Alexey)
item.description: Two languages that look like recipes — but only one of them helps you cook dinner. A light-hearted comparison of Chef, the joke programming language, and Cooklang.

item.title: 6 Recipe File Formats Compared: Cooklang, Markdown, JSON-LD & More
item.author: hello@cooklang.org (Alexey)
item.description: A side-by-side comparison of recipe file formats — Cooklang, plain Markdown, Schema.org JSON-LD, MealMaster, RecipeML, and Open Recipe Format. The same recipe in each format, with pros, cons, and when to use each.

item.title: The Best Open Source Recipe Managers in 2026
item.author: hello@cooklang.org (Alexey)
item.description: A practical comparison of the best open source recipe managers — Cooklang, Mealie, KitchenOwl, Tandoor, and more. What each does well, where they fall short, and which one fits your workflow.

item.title: Best Recipe Management Software in 2026: 9 Apps Compared
item.author: hello@cooklang.org (Alexey)
item.description: A practical guide to the best recipe management software in 2026 — from polished commercial apps like Paprika to self-hosted tools like Mealie to plain-text approaches like Cooklang. Honest about trade-offs.

item.title: Mealie vs Paprika vs KitchenOwl vs Cooklang: Recipe Manager Comparison (2026)
item.author: hello@cooklang.org (Alexey)
item.description: Comparing four popular recipe management solutions — the open-source self-hosted Mealie, the polished commercial Paprika, the household-friendly KitchenOwl, and the plain-text Cooklang. An honest look at what each does best and which fits your cooking workflow.
